images.caption: Figure 1:Token teachability.A:Low-entropy, high-KL tokens can contain learnable disagreementDLD^{L}, which stays within the student’s local support, and incompatible disagreementDID^{I}, which shifts off support.B:TA-OPD computes disagreementDtD_{t}and compatibilityCtC_{t}, ranks positions bystteach=D~t​C~ts_{t}^{\mathrm{teach}}=\tilde{D}_{t}\tilde{C}_{t}, and keeps only high-teachability OPD supervision.

Figure 2:Local-support decomposition.A: fixed-context gain over learnable disagreementDLD^{L}and incompatible disagreementDID^{I}.
B–C:DLD^{L}andDID^{I}projected onto TIP’s entropy–KL plane; Q3 denotes the low-entropy/high-KL region.
Teachability separates support-aligned corrections from off-support mismatch within Q3.

Figure 3:Fixed-context evidence for token teachability.A:DLD^{L}has about twice the standardized coefficient ofDID^{I}.
B: within Q3, high-DLD^{L}tokens are beneficial, while low-DLD^{L}and high-DID^{I}tokens are weak or harmful.
C: high-support gain gaps remain positive across held-out contexts, GSM8K-COT, larger teachers, and support sizes.

Figure 4:Q3 controls.A–B: exact top-NNcomparisons under matched token counts; B reports gain per kept token.
C: support proxies yield positive high–low gain gaps inside Q3 atK=16,32K=16,32.
D: bucket trends (thin: individual diagnostics; bold: mean) show support mass tracks gain, whereas raw KL andDID^{I}decline.
